papers.abstract: By introducing diallyl disulfide to regulate and stabilize the perovskite films through intramolecular chemical rearrangement, the corresponding device achieved 26.08% of efficiency with improved photothermal stability.

papers.title: Phase homogeneity mediated charge-carrier balance in two-step-method halide perovskite photovoltaics
papers.abstract: Phase homogeneity mediated charge-carrier balance reveals the heterogeneity issue of two-step-method perovskite films.

papers.title: Homogenizing SAM deposition via seeding –OH groups for scalable fabrication of perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Seeding –OH groups on substrates via an H 2 O 2 /UV bath helps to homogenize and cure SAM adsorption for scalable fabrication of perovskite solar cells with impressive efficiency and stability.

papers.title: Design of strong and weak intermolecular interactions to engineer buried interfaces in inverted wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Low-contact-loss and durable buried interface was engineering by a molecular hybridization strategy. The proton transfer from Me-4PACz to Histamine enables distinguished efficiency and operational stability of PSCs based on mix-halide perovskites.

papers.title: Enhanced electrical performance of perovskite solar cells via strain engineering
papers.abstract: Strain heterogeneity in perovskite films impacts the energy landscape, affecting charge transport. Incorporating 2TI molecules modifies strain distribution, enhancing charge transport and achieving 25.96% efficiency with enhanced stability.

papers.title: Local phase-modulated heterostructures for perovskite solar cells with high-efficiency and ultra-stability
papers.abstract: An asymmetric Lewis base organic semiconductor, CY, was incorporated into the perovskite layer, enabling a local phase-modulated heterostructure that achieves 26.0% efficiency and excellent stability in perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Radical scavenger-driven oxidation prevention and structural stabilization for efficient and stable tin-based perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: TEMPOL serves as a radical scavenger that suppresses Sn 2+ oxidation, facilitates controlled perovskite growth, and improves charge transport at the HTL/perovskite interface, thereby enhancing the performance and stability of Sn-based PSCs.

papers.title: In situ molecular compensation in wide-bandgap perovskites for efficient all-perovskite tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: 5-AVAI, with its larger dipole and amphoteric nature, can effectively compensate for SAM-deficient interfaces and GB defects in WBG perovskite sub-cells, leading to efficient tandems.

papers.title: Anti-solvent engineering for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells with preferentially orientated 2-dimensional/3-dimensional heterojunctions
papers.abstract: A (001) facet out-of-plane-oriented 2D/3D perovskite was achieved by introducing 4-MeO-PEAI and n -HeXAI into hybrid anti-solvents, exhibiting a PCE of 26.02% and V OC of 1.218 V.

papers.title: Robust interfaces via synergistic cation–anion manipulation for highly efficient and stable inverted perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: A molecular passivation strategy based on synergistic cation–anion manipulation effectively modulates interfacial defects, achieving a PCE of 26.10% with outstanding long-term stability.

papers.title: Balancing the supersaturation rate and coordination capability for upscaling high-performance perovskite photovoltaics
papers.abstract: Coupling in situ monitoring with supersaturation-rate calculations, we employ a facile solvent-engineering strategy to balance supersaturation rate and coordination capability, enabling 25.38% PSCs (0.09 cm 2 ) and a 23.22% mini-module (21.84 cm 2 ).

papers.title: Surface-deprotonated ultra-small SnO 2 quantum dots for high-performance perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: SnO 2 electron transport layers (ETLs) have significantly boosted the recent record efficiencies in perovskite solar cells (PSCs).

papers.title: Ultrasonic spraying of Ce(Mn,Fe)O 2 nanocatalysts onto a perovskite surface for highly efficient electrochemical CO 2 reduction
papers.abstract: We propose an ultrasonic spraying strategy for the one-step fabrication of uniform nano-electrodes with a nano-convex structure on an all-ceramic fuel electrode, enhancing both activity and durability.

papers.title: Efficient rigid and flexible perovskite solar cells using strongly adsorbed molecules for lattice repair and grain boundary mitigation
papers.abstract: Molecular passivation reduces the lattice defects and induces large grains for high efficiencies of perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Multi-physics mechanisms and regulation of perovskite grain boundaries: insights into carrier dynamics, ion migration, thermodynamics, and thermal stress
papers.abstract: This study investigates the multi-physics mechanisms of grain boundaries in perovskite solar cells by performing opto-electro-thermal-stress coupled simulations, with analyses from carrier transport, ion migration, thermodynamics, and thermal stress.

papers.title: A universal strategy toward homogenized metal oxide/perovskite contact for efficient solar cells and modules fabricated in ambient air
papers.abstract: A universal hydrazide strategy enables homogenized metal oxide/perovskite contact and dual-side passivation for air-processed solar cells and modules.

papers.title: High-performance inverted perovskite solar cells and modules via aminothiazole passivation
papers.abstract: A multifunctional additive 5ATCl is explored to passivate the undesirable defects of perovskites, which enables high-quality perovskite films and thus demonstrates impressive V OC × FF value for both rigid and flexible inverted perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: A dual-site additive mediated crystallization strategy for homogenized FA–Cs based perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: In situ analyses reveal that component segregation originates from non-equilibrium crystallization induced by differential coordination. A dual-site mediation strategy homogenizes the film, boosting performance, stability, and scalability.

papers.title: Biomass-derived functional additive for highly efficient and stable lead halide perovskite solar cells with built-in lead immobilisation
papers.abstract: The functional biomass additive TBA-Alg simultaneously improves the PCE, stability and lead immobility of lead halide perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Mitigating the amorphization of perovskite layers by using atomic layer deposition of alumina
papers.abstract: Schematic depiction of spiro-OMeTAD degradation causing perovskite instability under pseudo-operating conditions and its prevention using an ultra-thin ALD layer of alumina.

papers.title: Two-terminal perovskite/Cu(In,Ga)Se 2 tandems with conformal coatings based on commercial bottom cells with >26% efficiency
papers.abstract: Employing DMF-assisted I-2PACz reconstruction and a BSSS strategy improves conformal SAM/perovskite coverage and C60 adsorption, enabling a record 26.14% (certified 25.21%) tandem efficiency on commercial CIGS.

papers.title: Dual-site passivation by heterocycle functionalized amidinium cations toward high-performance inverted perovskite solar cells and modules
papers.abstract: This work reports a dual-site passivation of anionic and cationic defects through heterocycle functionalized amidinium cations for achieving highly efficient perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Unveiling the single-solvent vacuum-flash process for high-efficiency perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: A single-solvent vacuum-flash process enables precise crystallization control, resulting in perovskite solar cells with a champion efficiency of 26.93% (certified at 26.78%).

papers.title: Metastable interphase induced pre-strain compensation enables efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: We developed a pre-strain compensation strategy by introducing a metastable interphase at the buried interface to release the thermal-induced residual tensile strain in perovskite films, leading to highly efficient and stable perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Redox mediator-modified self-assembled monolayer stabilizes a buried interface in efficient inverted perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: The redox material methylene blue was utilized to construct a selective redox cycle at the NiO x /perovskite interface and optimize the dispersion of SAMs through π–π interactions, resulting in high-efficiency PSCs with excellent operational stability.

papers.title: Buried interface modification for high performance and stable perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Phenylhydrazinium chloride (PC) is used as the buried interface modification material of a perovskite film to passivate defects and retard the crystallization process of perovskite films, resulting in improved PCE from 24.67% to 25.80%.

papers.title: Charge carrier management for highly efficient perovskite/Si tandem solar cells with poly-Si based passivating contacts
papers.abstract: A thermal annealing treatment applied to the silicon bottom cell as well as a morphology regulation strategy of buried interface are employed to improve charge carrier management in perovskite/POLO-Si tandem solar cells, achieving a PCE of 31%.

papers.title: A versatile energy-level-tunable hole-transport layer for multi-composition inverted perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Through a bimolecular energy-level-tunable design, an ET-HTL reaches optimal energy level alignment with three different perovskite compositions, providing balanced interface defect passivation, charge extraction, and transition loss suppression.

papers.title: Enhanced interface adhesion with a polymeric hole transporter enabling high-performance air-processed perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: A polymeric hole transporter is developed with enhanced adhesion to the substrate and improved conductivity. Our air-processed perovskite solar cells achieve a power conversion efficiency of ∼25% and demonstrate good stability.

papers.title: Interface parallel dipole regulation in all-perovskite tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: POEAX (X = I, Br, Cl) parallel dipole engineering simultaneously passivates defects and modulates interfaces in wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells, achieving 19.54% efficiency and enabling all-perovskite tandem solar cells with 28.92% efficiency.

papers.title: Efficient charge separation at localized 2D ferroelectric domains in perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: Ferroelectric properties can be utilized for efficient charge carrier separation by spontaneous electric polarization.

papers.title: Tailored self-assembled monolayer molecules for perovskite/PERC tandem solar cells with efficiencies over 30%
papers.abstract: The fluorine substitution strategy, with asymmetric structure and larger dipole moment, enhances charge transport and stabilizes the perovskite/SAM interface. A cost-effective perovskite/PERC tandem solar cell achieved a record efficiency of 30.05%.

papers.title: Unraveling the interfacial homogeneity and bulk crystallization for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells via ionic liquids
papers.abstract: Achieving the interfacial homogeneity and bulk crystallization for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells via ionic liquids.

papers.title: Efficient and stable inverted MA/Br-free 2D/3D perovskite solar cells enabled by α -to- δ phase transition inhibition and crystallization modulation
papers.abstract: An FA-based organic derivative, namely BLSCl, has been explored as a spacer for MA/Br-free FA-based 2D/3D perovskites. A champion efficiency of 25.96% for the 0.1 cm 2 device and 24.54% (certified 23.94%) for the 1.01 cm 2 device have been achieved.

papers.title: A spatial structure regulation strategy modulated the solubility and compactness of novel face-on oriented bisphosphonate-anchored SAMs for efficient inverted perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: A spatial structure regulation strategy was used to achieve a remarkable PCE of 25.81%, which represents the highest efficiency for PSCs using novel face-on oriented bisphosphonate-anchored self-assembled molecules as HTLs.

papers.title: From 20% single-junction organic photovoltaics to 26% perovskite/organic tandem solar cells: self-assembled hole transport molecules matter
papers.abstract: The stacking of SAM layers was regulated to form “face-on” orientation via incorporating the volatile solid additive TCB.

papers.title: pH-Independent lead sequestration and light management enable sustainable and efficient perovskite photovoltaics
papers.abstract: Textured phosphate-buffered functionalized polymer films are designed to mitigate the lead leakage and optical loss challenges of perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Suppressing phase segregation and nonradiative losses by a multifunctional cross-linker for high-performance all-perovskite tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: A cross-linker is designed to suppress the notorious light-induced phase segregation and nonradiative losses. The as-fabricated WBG PSCs deliver a certified V OC of 1.37 V, which is among the highest values for WBG PSCs. And it enables 28.3% APTSCs.

papers.title: Face-on oriented self-assembled molecules with enhanced π–π stacking for highly efficient inverted perovskite solar cells on rough FTO substrates
papers.abstract: 4PABCz molecules with asymmetric structures form dense coverage on FTO substrates owing to their robust multilayered structure with strong intermolecular π–π stacking and face-on configuration for efficient charge transport.

papers.title: A spiro-type self-assembled hole transporting monolayer for highly efficient and stable inverted perovskite solar cells and modules
papers.abstract: The twisted spiro-type SAM 4PA-spiro effectively suppresses molecular aggregation and ensures appropriate energy levels, providing an efficiency of 25.28 and 21.87% for the p–i–n PSCs (0.05 cm 2 ) and modules (29.0 cm 2 ).

papers.title: Over one-micron-thick void-free perovskite layers enable highly efficient and fully printed solar cells
papers.abstract: Guided by phase-field simulations, a pre-coated 2D perovskite layer enables the growth of void-free perovskite layers over one-micron-thick, achieving high-efficiency, fully printed solar cells.

papers.title: All-inorganic CsPbI 2 Br perovskite solar cells with thermal stability at 250 °C and moisture-resilience via polymeric protection layers
papers.abstract: All-inorganic perovskites, such as CsPbI 2 Br, have emerged as promising compositions due to their enhanced thermal stability. However, they are very prone to degradation due to moisture.

papers.title: Halogen anion pre-homogenization of sequentially deposited wide bandgap perovskites for commercial textured perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: A halogen pre-homogenizing approach enables a perovskite/Si tandem module of 64.64 cm 2 achieving a photoelectric conversion efficiency of 27.1%.

papers.title: 23.2% efficient low band gap perovskite solar cells with cyanogen management
papers.abstract: The role of thiocyanates in minimising organohalide diffusion into PEDOT:PSS whilst accelerating device degradation is identified and a route towards improving both device efficiency and stability is demonstrated.

papers.title: Surpassing 90% Shockley–Queisser V OC limit in 1.79 eV wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells using bromine-substituted self-assembled monolayers
papers.abstract: DCB-Br-2 is used as a hole-transporting layer for 1.79 eV wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells. Its stronger interaction with the perovskite and better energy level alignment enable the achievement of a notable V OC of 1.37 V.

papers.title: Boron–halide interactions for crystallization regulation of a 1.68 eV wide-bandgap perovskite prepared via a two-step method
papers.abstract: Leveraging tris(pentafluorophenyl)borane (BCF) to regulate crystallization of perovskite by the boron–halogen and hydrogen bonding effects enabled a PCE of 23.49% and 31.12% at 1.68 eV for single-junction PSCs and perovskite/Si TSCs, respectively.

papers.title: Damp-heat stable and efficient perovskite solar cells and mini-modules with a t BP-free hole-transporting layer
papers.abstract: The EC electrolyte, replacing t BP, enables LiTFSI dissolution and generates p-doped radicals. It achieves a peak efficiency of 25.56% for PSCs, expanding the viability of large-area PSMs and enhancing long-term stability under damp-heat conditions.

papers.title: Self-assembled metal cluster/perovskite catalysts for efficient acidic hydrogen production with an ultra-low overpotential of 62 mV and over 1500 hours of stability at 1 A cm −2
papers.abstract: This work presents an excellent HER catalyst with promising industrial application and reveals the in situ reconstruction processes and formation of active species of Ca 2 CoRuO 6 .

papers.title: Anchoring ligand engineering enables highly stable MA-free perovskite solar cells with a minimal V OC deficit of 0.32 V
papers.abstract: Ligand engineering is an effective method to reduce defects in perovskite solar cells (PSCs) and to enhance efficiency.

papers.title: Stretching the future: strategies and emerging trends in stretchable organic photovoltaic materials
papers.abstract: This review consolidates the latest advancements in stretchable organic photovoltaic (s-OPV) materials, providing a comprehensive overview of the field's progress.

papers.title: Upcycling waste photovoltaic cells into silicon carbide via flash Joule heating
papers.abstract: Flash Joule heating rapidly converts waste silicon solar cells into high purity SiC at ∼2200 °C, offering a low-emission, energy-efficient, and cost-effective route for sustainable PV waste upcycling.

papers.title: Enhanced moisture and thermal stability of transparent electrodes via crosslinking for large-area flexible organic photovoltaic modules
papers.abstract: Crosslinking enhanced the thermal and moisture stability of the AgNWs-em-PVA flexible transparent electrode, which enabled the fabrication of large-area (52.3 cm 2 ), flexible organic photovoltaic modules with a power conversion efficiency of 14.78%.

papers.title: Conversion of photovoltaic waste silicon into amorphous silicon nanowire anodes
papers.abstract: A high-performance self-supporting electrode was developed that converts photovoltaic waste silicon into amorphous silicon nanowires.

papers.title: Simultaneously improving efficiency, stability and intrinsic stretchability of organic photovoltaic films via molecular toughening
papers.abstract: A tethered molecule is introduced to realize intrinsically stretchable organic photovoltaics (OPVs) with over 15% efficiency via benign solvents, and thickness-dependent mechanical properties is elucidated for the first time in OPVs.

papers.title: Long term outdoor performance evaluation of printed semitransparent organic photovoltaic modules for BIPV/BAPV applications
papers.abstract: Outdoor monitoring of Roll-to-Roll (R2R) printed flexible organic photovoltaic (OPV) modules for building integrated/attached photovoltaics (BIPV/BAPV) applications.

papers.title: Revolutionizing the solar photovoltaic efficiency: a comprehensive review on the cutting-edge thermal management methods for advanced and conventional solar photovoltaics
papers.abstract: Innovative PETS enhance PV efficiency and support low-carbon goals, yet gaps remain on feasibility, commercialization, and cooling specifics. This review shows PETS can boost efficiency upto 50% with cooling, highlighting future 4E studies.

papers.title: Simultaneously improving the efficiencies of organic photovoltaic devices and modules by finely manipulating the aggregation behaviors of Y-series molecules
papers.abstract: We have developed a novel liquid additive that boosts J -aggregation in NFAs through enhanced non-covalent interactions with the BTP core, leading to a record efficiency for thick-film (≥200 nm), large-area OPV modules.

papers.title: Strategies to improve the photovoltaic performance of M-series acceptor-based polymer solar cells: chemical hybridization versus physical blending of acceptors
papers.abstract: A novel asymmetric M-series acceptor (M36-FCl) with a large dielectric constant and a smaller exciton dissociation barrier has been developed for high-performance polymer solar cells.

papers.title: Manipulating crystallization kinetics and vertical phase distribution via a small molecule donor guest for organic photovoltaic cells with 20% efficiency
papers.abstract: A kinetics-controlling strategy is used to regulate molecular crystallization and vertical phase distribution, where a third small molecule donor is utilized to advance the aggregation of polymer donor and prolong its self-assembly time.

papers.title: Molecular design of high-performance wide-bandgap acceptor enables versatile organic photovoltaic applications
papers.abstract: A low-cost wide-bandgap acceptor FPCC-Br was designed which exhibited a champion PCE of 13.6% in OPV cells below 720 nm and demonstrated significant advantages in indoor lighting, tandem cells, and underwater photovoltaic electrolysis applications.

papers.title: Synergistic cooperation between photovoltaic and thermoelectric effects in solar cells
papers.abstract: A record power conversion efficiency of 27.17% was achieved in the photovoltaic cells under a temperature gradient of 10 °C, enabled by synergistic cooperation between photovoltaic and thermoelectric effects.

papers.title: Suppressing electrostatic potential fluctuations to achieve high-efficiency organic photovoltaic cells for laser wireless energy transfer
papers.abstract: The wide-bandgap acceptor AITO-2F was synthesized to explore the impact of molecular aggregation on electrostatic potential fluctuations, achieving remarkable PCE and FF under AM 1.5G and 660 nm laser illumination.

papers.title: Analysis of the charge generation and recombination processes in the PM6:Y6 organic solar cell
papers.abstract: This work gives a comprehensive description of the fundamental electronic processes taking place in the active layer of PM6:Y6 solar cells. It also points to the role of triplet states, excimer-like states, and defect states in device performance.

papers.title: Semi-transparent solar cells: strategies for maximum power output in cities
papers.abstract: Despite tinted transmission, semi-transparent solar cells using the band selective method exhibit higher performance at similar transparency levels, with PCE (28% vs. 22%) and LUE (23% vs. 19%), thus higher power output in empirical city irradiance.

papers.title: One more step towards better stability of non-fullerene organic solar cells: advances, challenges, future perspectives, and the Era of artificial intelligence
papers.abstract: This review summarizes the recent progress, challenges, and future perspective towards stable non-fullerene organic solar cells (NF-OSCs) with detailed discussion of various aspects and effective strategies to improve the stability of NF-OSCs.

papers.title: Fully evaporated interfacial layers for high-performance and batch-to-batch reproducible organic solar modules
papers.abstract: Significant advancements in research have been made in recent years, with single-junction organic solar cells achieving efficiencies exceeding 20%.

papers.title: Solar-driven water splitting with ascorbic acid oxidation for efficient hydrogen production
papers.abstract: By integrating the AAOR with the HER in a solar-powered membrane-free system, this research achieves a 3.5-fold reduction in energy consumption compared to conventional electrolysis while generating additional economic value from DHA production.

papers.title: Highly efficient all-small-molecule organic solar cells with excellent operational stability and blend-thickness tolerance
papers.abstract: Highly efficient ternary all-SMOSCs are developed by a donor alloy strategy, which deliver a record-high PCE of 18.51%. They also exhibit excellent operational stability and blend-thickness tolerance.

papers.title: 20.0% efficiency of ternary organic solar cells enabled by a novel wide band gap polymer guest donor
papers.abstract: A novel wide bandgap polymer donor, P(BTzE-BDT), was synthesized and incorporated into the PM6:BTP-eC9 system to fabricate ternary OSCs, achieving a higher power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 20.0%.

papers.title: Comprehensive crystallization retardation of inorganic perovskites for high performance inverted solar cells
papers.abstract: Acrylonitrile–methyl acrylate is added to the perovskite precursor to retard the crystallization of inorganic perovskites, yielding a record efficiency of 21.7% for inverted inorganic PSCs, together with substantially improved operational stability.

papers.title: Non-fused core-linked star-shaped oligomer acceptors for stable binary organic solar cells with over 19% efficiency
papers.abstract: Non-fused core with non-covalent interactions refines the film-forming kinetics of star-shaped oligomer acceptors for achieving long-term stable solar cells with 19.27% efficiency.

papers.title: Achieving unprecedented power-output in 4-terminal mirror-symmetrical printable carbon CsPbBr 3 solar cells through dual-solvent engineering
papers.abstract: Tyndall effect-guided dual-solvent engineering optimized printable carbon-based CsPbBr 3 solar cells, achieving a record PCE of 10.18%. A 4-terminal concentrator enhanced output to 29.44 mW cm −2 , with 93.2% PCE retention after 1000 hours at 150 °C.

papers.title: Balanced distribution of donors and acceptors enabled by volatile isomerization additives for 20.81% efficiency layer-by-layer polymer solar cells
papers.abstract: Volatile isomerization additives induce a vertical phase separation morphology with balanced distribution of donor and acceptor as well as excellent carrier dynamics, enabling the maximum efficiency of 20.81% in layer-by-layer polymer solar cells.

papers.title: Dynamic disulfide bond networks enable self-healable and mechanically resilient intrinsically stretchable organic solar cells
papers.abstract: A dynamic disulfide network introduced into donor/acceptor blends enables room-temperature self-healing and mechanical resilience in intrinsically stretchable organic solar cells, achieving performance recovery after high mechanical strain.

papers.title: High efficiency of solution-processed inverted organic solar cells enabled by an aluminum oxide conjunction structure
papers.abstract: A solution-processed hole transport layer (HTL) in conjunction with an aluminum oxide (Al 2 O 3 ) buffer layer has been developed to achieve efficient inverted organic solar cells.

papers.title: A flowing liquid phase induces the crystallization processes of cesium lead triiodide for 21.85%-efficiency solar cells and low-energy loss
papers.abstract: The PCE of AFMS passivated CsPbI 3 PSCs reached 21.85%, which is the highest for this type of pure cesium lead triiodide solar cell.

papers.title: Dielectric constant engineering of nonfullerene acceptors enables a record fill factor of 83.58% and a high efficiency of 20.80% in organic solar cells
papers.abstract: Two nonfullerene acceptors with trifluoromethyl end-capped alkyl side chains increased the dielectric constant and optimized optoelectronic properties, achieving an impressive power conversion efficiency of 20.80% in organic solar cells.

papers.title: Arbitrary and active colouring of solar cells with negligible loss of efficiency
papers.abstract: Self-assembled cholesteric liquid crystals uniquely enable non-spectral coloured and thermochromic covered highly efficient photovoltaic devices for aesthetic integration.

papers.title: A halogenated volatile additive strategy for regulating crystallization kinetics and enabling 20.40% efficiency polymer solar cells with low non-radiative recombination energy loss
papers.abstract: Increasing the weight of halogen atoms of additives results in balanced crystallization and optimal morphology with suppressed energy disorder, realizing 20.40% efficiency with a Δ E non-rad of 0.189 eV and E loss of 0.500 eV in polymer solar cells.

papers.title: Dispersion effect of chlorination-induced intermolecular stacking optimization of small-molecule acceptors for high-performance organic solar cells
papers.abstract: We demonstrated the significance of a polarizable dispersion effect of a chlorine substituent in optimizing the intermolecular π–π stacking of a small-molecule acceptor for efficient organic solar cells.

papers.title: Binary all-polymer solar cells with 19.30% efficiency enabled by bromodibenzothiophene-based solid additive
papers.abstract: Two volatile solid additives were developed to modulate the active-layer morphology of all-polymer solar cells (all-PSCs). Among them, the 4-BDBTP-treated all-PSC achieved an outstanding efficiency of 19.30%.

papers.title: A large conjugated rigid dimer acceptor enables 20.19% efficiency in organic solar cells
papers.abstract: A dimer acceptor QD-1 with a large conjugated rigid skeleton is reported, showing low disorder, weak electro-photon coupling, and thus low non-radiative recombination loss. An efficiency of 20.19% is achieved for the QD-1-based ternary device.

papers.title: Balanced electron and hole transfer behavior enabled approaching 19% efficiency in thick-film organic solar cells with improved fill factor
papers.abstract: In this study, a high FF was achieved in thick-film OSCs with a thickness of 300 nm by balancing electron and hole transfer, yielding a high power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 18.92%.

papers.title: Fine-tuning central extended unit symmetry via atom-level asymmetric molecular design enables efficient binary organic solar cells
papers.abstract: An atom-level asymmetric molecular design strategy was proposed to develop and synthesize two asymmetric acceptors, CH-Bzq and CH-Bzq-Br. The PM6:CH-Bzq-Br-based binary device achieves an impressive efficiency of 19.42%.

papers.title: Molecular order manipulation with dual additives suppressing trap density in non-fullerene acceptors enables efficient bilayer organic solar cells
papers.abstract: Using dual additives balanced the molecular aggregation size and order, which led to a flocculent acceptor layer morphology that suppressed the trap density, energetic disorder, and recombination rate in efficient bilayer organic solar cells.

papers.title: Levelized cost and carbon intensity of solar hydrogen production via water splitting using a scalable and intrinsically safe photocatalytic Z-scheme raceway system
papers.abstract: Schematic of photocatalytic type 2 Z-scheme raceway design with hydrogen reactor cylinders floating on an oxygen reactor raceway pool. The raceway concept enables a scalable, low-cost, and low-carbon intensity method of hydrogen production.

papers.title: Opportunities and challenges for emerging inorganic chalcogenide–silicon tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: This review highlights the potential of emerging inorganic chalcogenide–silicon tandem solar cells, which address efficiency and stability limitations of single-junction devices and offer a less-toxic, more stable alternative for tandem applications.

papers.title: Light radiation annealing enables unidirectional crystallization of vacuum-assisted Sn–Pb perovskites for efficient tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: Vacuum-assisted technology, favored for its low cost, environmentally friendly nature, and scalability, is widely used in perovskite solar cells and is a preferred choice for commercialized production.

papers.title: Crystal phase and band edge modulation of MA- and Br-free CsFA-based perovskites for efficient inverted solar cells and minimodules
papers.abstract: A Lewis base is selected to regulate crystal growth, reduce δ-phase formation and inhibit halide oxidation of perovskites, enabling champion efficiencies of 26.12% for a 0.09-cm 2 inverted device and 22.67% for a 12.96-cm 2 minimodule, respectively.

papers.title: Achieving highly efficient, mechanically robust and thermally stable organic solar cells through optimizing the branching position and side chain length of small molecule acceptors
papers.abstract: Highly efficient, mechanically robust, and thermally stable OSCs have been developed by optimizing the branching position and side chain length of SMAs, marking a significant advancement in the field of intrinsically stretchable OSCs.

papers.title: 19.46%-Efficiency all-polymer organic solar cells with excellent outdoor operating stability enabled by active layer reconstruction
papers.abstract: Layer-by-layer blade deposition was employed along with a multi-component strategy to construct a quaternary all-polymer system, achieving a high power conversion efficiency of 19.46% and improved outdoor operating stability.

papers.title: Double amidino-mediated multiple hydrogen-bonded Dion–Jacobson perovskites enable oriented crystallization for efficient inverted FAPbI 3 solar cells and modules (642 cm 2 )
papers.abstract: A double amidino-based derivative, namely PhDFACl 2 , has been explored as a spacer for DJ 2D/3D FAPbI 3 -based perovskites. Champion efficiencies of 26.10%, 24.81%, and 18.20% for 0.1 cm 2 , 1.01 cm 2 , and 642 cm 2 devices have been achieved.

papers.title: Direct C–H arylation-derived low crystallinity guest acceptor for high efficiency organic solar cells
papers.abstract: This work provides valuable guidelines for the exploration of guest materials via the C–H activation reaction, while controlling the crystallization kinetics to fine-tune the assembly behavior of the host acceptor for enhanced device performance.

papers.title: Halogen-substituted phenazine cores reduce energy losses and optimize carrier dynamics in tethered acceptors for 19.8% efficient and stable polymer solar cells
papers.abstract: The thermodynamic relaxation and rigidity of small-molecule acceptors drive an oligomeric design approach in polymer solar cells, achieving device efficiencies approaching 20% through tailored molecular engineering.

papers.title: The butterfly-effect of flexible linkers in giant-molecule acceptors: optimized crystallization and aggregation for enhancing mechanical durability and approaching 19% efficiency in binary organic solar cells
papers.abstract: Butterfly-like conformation and optimization of crystallinity and aggregation of molecules enable giant molecular acceptors (GMAs) to achieve a balance between photovoltaic and mechanical properties of flexible organic solar cells.

papers.title: Central core asymmetric acceptor design enables over 20% efficiency in binary organic solar cells by suppressing non-radiative energy loss and optimizing nanomorphology
papers.abstract: Two acceptors Ph-2F and Ph-2Cl are designed via core-asymmetric substitution and halogenated engineering. Ph-2F based organic solar cells (OSCs) achieve an efficiency of 20.33%, which is the highest value for OSCs based on asymmetric acceptors.

papers.title: Freezing the p–i–n interlayer with a symmetric phenolic compound to achieve favorable vertical morphology and efficient pseudo-planar heterojunction organic solar cells with excellent stability
papers.abstract: A strategy of using symmetric phenolic compound 4,4′-dihydroxybiphenyl to freeze p–i–n interlayer of pseudo-planar heterojunction organic solar cells has been first developed, resulting in optimized and stable vertical phase separation morphology.

papers.title: Efficient organic solar cells with a printed p–i–n stack enabled by an azeotrope-processed self-assembled monolayer
papers.abstract: Azeotrope-assisted self-assembled monolayer printing boosts efficiency across all slot-die coated p–i–n organic solar cells.

papers.title: Asymmetric non-fullerene acceptors with balanced crystallization kinetics enabling a trade-off between charge generation and recombination in ternary organic solar cells
papers.abstract: The ability of asymmetric ternary components to regulate crystallization kinetics enables a trade-off between charge generation and recombination, achieving synchronized improvements in both V OC and J SC .

papers.title: Reducing energy loss by developing luminescent triphenylamine functionalized electron acceptor for high performance organic solar cells
papers.abstract: A fused non-fullerene acceptor, Z-Tri, with a luminescent triphenylamine unit, forms a mixed phase with enhanced PLQY, lower Δ E nr , and higher V oc in ternary devices.

papers.title: Induced oriented attachment of a ZnO electron transport layer enables over 20% efficiency in solution-processed conventional organic solar cells
papers.abstract: Dual-functional solid additives induce oriented attachment of ZnO nanoparticles, simultaneously regulating nanoparticle ordering and defect passivation, enabling record efficiencies in both rigid and flexible organic solar cells.

papers.title: Breaking the symmetry of interfacial molecules with push–pull substituents enables 19.67% efficiency organic solar cells featuring enhanced charge extraction
papers.abstract: The unsymmetrical push–pull strategy in the interfacial layer enables fast charge extraction and suppresses surface and trap-assisted recombination, achieving a 19.67% efficiency in binary single junction organic solar cells.

papers.title: Elucidating the role of heterojunction in pristine non-fullerene acceptor organic solar cells
papers.abstract: This study provides direct evidence that pristine L8BO exhibits interfacial charge generation, challenging prior Y6 studies on intrinsic free charge generation.

papers.title: 26.35%-Efficiency and high-bifaciality n-TOPCon solar cells enabled by UV-ps laser-induced selective modification of double-layered SiO x /n + -poly-Si passivating contacts
papers.abstract: Double-layered SiO x /n + -poly-Si films with laterally modulated thickness applied in TOPCon solar cells can reduced recombination, enhance contact quality, decrease risk of paste burn-through of the passivation layer and reduce parasitic absorption.

papers.title: Achieving 20% efficiency in binary organic solar cells with suppressed non-radiative recombination via triphenylamine halides
papers.abstract: A series of high fluorescence triphenylamine halides are proposed as solid additives to reduce the static disorder and thus improve the luminescence performance of the active layer to suppress non-radiative recombination loss.

papers.title: Constructing a dual-fiber network in high efficiency organic solar cells via additive-induced supramolecular interactions with both donor and acceptor
papers.abstract: All additives ( TZ-Cl , TZ-2Cl , TZ-3Cl ) can facilitate the formation of a bicontinuous network structure in active layer through supramolecular interactions, and TZ-3Cl achieved PCEs of 19.8% and 20.2% in binary/ternary OSCs.

papers.title: Amorphous indium–zinc oxide layer with transport and protective dual-function for high-efficiency semitransparent and tandem Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cells
papers.abstract: Bifunctional IZO was soft-sputtered for semitransparent Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cells, achieving the highest efficiency both in semitransparent and all-antimony-based tandem devices.

papers.title: Nucleation driving force-controlled fibril network formation using a non-halogenated solvent enables polythiophene solar cells with over 18% efficiency
papers.abstract: Classical Nucleation Theory guides fibril network formation in polythiophene-based photoactive blend using a non-halogenated solvent. Solar cells thus achieved 18.12% efficiency and 79.17% fill factor, both record-high for polythiophenes.

papers.title: “Head surgery” of polycyclic o -quinones with cyanated aromatic rings towards high electron mobility acceptors enables 19.6% efficiency in additive-free binary organic solar cells
papers.abstract: New acceptors were synthesized via an o -quinone-mediated cycloaromatization strategy. Additive-free organic solar cells with efficiency of 19.6% and organic field effect transistors with electron mobility of 1.1 cm 2 V −1 s −1 were realized.

papers.title: A bithiophene imide-based polymer donor for alloy-like ternary organic solar cells with over 20.5% efficiency and enhanced stability
papers.abstract: Ternary organic solar cells incorporating a bithiophene imide-based polymer donor in a dual-donor system achieve an outstanding efficiency of 20.52% and excellent thermal stability, retaining over 92.2% performance after 1008 h of heating.

papers.title: Phase evolution regulation of CZTSSe absorbers via a ZnO blocking layer enables 14.45% efficient kesterite solar cells with low V OC deficit
papers.abstract: The one-step phase evolution from CZTS to CZTSSe was realized through a ZnO blocking layer. This prevented the formation of detrimental secondary phases, resulting in an absorber with superior crystalline quality and reduced defect concentration.

papers.title: Exposing binding-favourable facets of perovskites for tandem solar cells
papers.abstract: Controlling the growth of polycrystalline perovskite films to achieve a favourable termination can significantly reduce interfacial losses and enhance the output voltage in wide-bandgap and all-perovskite tandem solar cells.

papers.title: Constructing continuous acceptor fibrillar networks and achieving uniform phase separation via polymer-assisted morphology control for 20.3% efficiency additive-free organic solar cells
papers.abstract: A small fraction of polymer acceptor was introduced as both nucleating agent and epitaxial growth template to enable small-molecule acceptor to form a continuous fibrillar network, achieving 20.3% efficiency in additive-free organic solar cells.

papers.title: Dissolution swelling effect-assisted interfacial morphology refinement enables high efficiency all-polymer solar cells
papers.abstract: Small molecule incorporation during sequential deposition improved donor crystallization and intercalation, optimizing the heterojunction morphology. The PCEs achieved 19.39% for small-area (0.04 cm 2 ) and 17.71% for large-area (1 cm 2 ) devices.

papers.title: Suppressed non-radiative loss and efficient hole transfer at a small highest occupied molecular orbital offset endows binary organic solar cells with 19.73% efficiency and a small efficiency-cost gap
papers.abstract: Two novel acceptors are designed and synthesized, and the PTQ11:PEH-F binary system is highly promising for industrial cost-effective organic photovoltaics.

papers.title: Three-dimensional bowl-shaped solid additive achieves 20.52% efficiency organic solar cells with enhanced thermal stability via curvature-mediated morphology regulation
papers.abstract: The incorporation of bowl-shaped corannulene as a solid additive in the active layer enhances organic solar cell efficiency to 20.52%, alongside remarkable thermal stability with a T 85 lifespan of 2007 h.

papers.title: Fullerene-embedded porphyrin metallacages as photoactive additives for stable binary organic solar cells with a certified efficiency of 20.2%
papers.abstract: Fullerene-embedded porphyrin metallacages are used as photoactive additives for the first time to manipulate the optoelectronic and morphological properties of binary organic solar cells for achieving a certified efficiency of 20.20%.

papers.title: Tailoring short-range mobility at donor–acceptor heterointerfaces through small molecules promotes efficient organic solar cells
papers.abstract: Understanding how to optimize the electronic processes and material selections are fundamental to fostering the development of organic solar cells (OSCs).

papers.title: Intrinsic point defect tolerance in selenium for indoor and tandem photovoltaics
papers.abstract: Intrinsic point defects are found to be inactive for electron–hole recombination, while extrinsic impurities do not contribute significantly to doping, pointing to extended defects and interfaces as limiting factors in selenium solar cells.

papers.title: Tailored on-surface fabrication of mesoporous metallic composites by direct pyrolysis of metal ion-accumulated micellar films for enhanced electrocatalytic water splitting
papers.abstract: A micellar film system was employed to direct the formation of highly tailored mesoporous metallic composites on eletrode.

papers.title: High-index facet NiFeB@H-NiFeBO core–shell nanowires for a highly efficient oxygen evolution reaction in water splitting
papers.abstract: A novel core–shell NiFeB@H-NiFeBO nanowire catalyst with an exposed high-index facet (HIF) outer layer simultaneously boosts OER stability and activity by activating lattice oxygen and stabilizing *OO-V O during the OER process.

papers.title: Dislocation-engineered piezocatalytic water splitting in single-crystal BaTiO 3
papers.abstract: Dislocations were introduced into BaTiO 3 single crystals and become catalytically active centers.

papers.title: Ultrafast synthesis of an efficient urea oxidation electrocatalyst for urea-assisted fast-charging Zn–air batteries and water splitting
papers.abstract: The urea oxidation reaction (UOR) efficiently treats urea-containing wastewater while replacing the high theoretical potential of the oxygen evolution reaction (OER), thereby enabling wastewater valorization.

papers.title: Developing low-resistance ion migration pathways using perfluorinated chain-decorated COFs for enhanced performance in zinc batteries
papers.abstract: Superhydrophobic fluorine chains was incorporated into covalent organic frameworks to engineer nanochannels that facilitate fast dehydration of ions and create low resistance ion migration pathways.

papers.title: Electrolytic cement clinker precursor production sustained through orthogonalization of ion vectors
papers.abstract: An electrochemical reactor (“cement electrolyser”) in tandem with a chemical reactor (“calcium reactor”) enables electrochemical production of Ca(OH) 2 for 50 hours at 100 mA cm −2 .

papers.title: Correction: Deciphering the interplay between tin vacancies and free carriers in the ion transport of tin-based perovskites
papers.abstract: Correction for ‘Deciphering the interplay between tin vacancies and free carriers in the ion transport of tin-based perovskites’ by Luis Huerta Hernandez et al. , Energy Environ. Sci. , 2025, 18 , 4787–4799, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5EE00632E.

papers.title: Deciphering the interplay between tin vacancies and free carriers in the ion transport of tin-based perovskites
papers.abstract: In this work, we demonstrate that a high concentration of hole carriers and tin vacancies facilitates the migration of iodide, enhancing ionic conductivity and mobile ion density in Sn-based halide perovskites.

papers.title: Improved efficiency and stability of outdoor and indoor organic photovoltaics with suppressed voltage loss via alkoxylation on dimeric giant acceptors featured as supramolecular stabilizers
papers.abstract: Alkoxylated dimeric giant acceptor DYO-V boosts organic solar cell efficiency and stability, achieving 20.2% PCE outdoors and 28.1% indoors, offering a versatile strategy for durable, high-performance photovoltaics.

papers.title: Sustainable perovskite light emitters
papers.abstract: This review highlights the balance across Pb-based, low-Pb, and Pb-free perovskite light emitters, focusing on sustainable applications from the perspectives of performance, toxicity, and ESG management.

papers.title: Surface chemistry-engineered perovskite quantum dot photovoltaics
papers.abstract: This review summarizes the progress and provides perspectives on perovskite quantum dot photovoltaics, with a focus on surface chemistry engineering, paving a new direction for large-area and low-cost PV technology to address major energy challenges.

papers.title: Supramolecular engineering in hybrid perovskite optoelectronics
papers.abstract: This review emphasizes how supramolecular engineering enhances perovskite optoelectronics by improving crystallization, morphology, stability, and performance, offering insights into recent advances and future potential for commercial applications.

papers.title: Sustainable decommissioning of perovskite solar cells: from waste to resources
papers.abstract: In this review, we discuss implications of the decommissioning of perovskite-based photovoltaics (PVs) from a sustainable perspective, reviewing current PSC recycling strategies as general guidelines for their end-of-life (EoL) management.

papers.title: Lattice strain inhomogeneity in halide perovskite films: its origins and regulations
papers.abstract: Since perovskite solar cells reached an astonishing power conversion efficiency of 27%, the persistent stability issue has received significant attention, paving the way for a major step up in commercialization.

papers.title: Dynamic control of halide perovskite structures for tailored ferroelectric and second-order nonlinear optical functionalities
papers.abstract: This review highlights the structural tunability of halide perovskites, strategies to enhance ferroelectric and second-order nonlinear optical properties, device integration, challenges in stability, scalability, and future research.

papers.title: From halide perovskite nanocrystals to supercrystals: fundamentals and applications
papers.abstract: Halide perovskite supercrystals (or superlattices) are electronically coupled nanomaterials, such as nanocrystals, quantum dots, or nanoplatelets that offer collective optical and electronic properties distinct from those of their constituents.

papers.title: Heteroatomic molecules for coordination engineering towards advanced Pb-free Sn-based perovskite photovoltaics
papers.abstract: Coordination engineering via developing various heteroatomic molecules has enabled Sn-based perovskite solar cells with efficiency approaching 16% and robust stability. This review summarizes the fundamentals, advances, and prospects of this topic.

papers.title: Molecular-level understandings and device strategies for FAPbI 3 -based perovskite solar cells
papers.abstract: The interaction between FA + and PbI 6 − octahedral frameworks is investigated, where the chemical bonding nature of inorganic framework determines the optoelectronic properties and structural stability.

papers.title: Correction: Decoding recombination dynamics in perovskite solar cells: an in-depth critical review
papers.abstract: Correction for ‘Decoding recombination dynamics in perovskite solar cells: an in-depth critical review’ by Ramkrishna Das Adhikari et al. , Chem. Soc. Rev. , 2025, 54 , 3962–4034, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4CS01231C.

papers.title: Decoding recombination dynamics in perovskite solar cells: an in-depth critical review
papers.abstract: This review systematically analyses the recombination pathways in PSCs, unveils cutting-edge suppression strategies, and underscores the potential of ML in optimizing device performance and expediting commercial integration.

papers.title: Single-crystal perovskites for photovoltaic and high-energy detection applications
papers.abstract: This review discusses single-crystal metal halide perovskites, including their growth methods, superior optoelectronic properties, applications in photovoltaics and high-energy radiation detection, and challenges toward commercialization.

papers.title: Emerging inorganic–organic hybrid photocatalysts for solar-driven overall water splitting: progress and perspectives
papers.abstract: This review explores emerging inorganic–organic hybrid photocatalysts that boost light harvesting, charge separation, and redox processes for overall water splitting, offering insights for sustainable H 2 production.

papers.title: Dynamic regulation of ferroelectric polarization using external stimuli for efficient water splitting and beyond
papers.abstract: Regulating the ferroelectric polarization in ferroelectric catalysts is an emerging strategy to advance water splitting reactions, with the merits of high charge transfer rate, creation of real active sites, and optimizing the chemisorption energy.

papers.title: Oblique‐Angle Damage‐Free Evaporation of Silicon Oxide Electron‐Selective Passivation Contacts for Efficient and Stable Perovskite and Perovskite/TOPCon Tandem Solar Cells (Adv. Energy Mater. 4/2025)
CeO 2 ‐Accelerated Surface Reconstruction of CoSe 2 Nanoneedle Forms Active CeO 2 @CoOOH Interface to Boost Oxygen Evolution Reaction for Water Splitting (Adv. Energy Mater. 4/2025)
Game-Changers for Flexible Perovskite Solar Cells and Modules: Elastomers and Cross-Linking Molecules
